name: Tamale Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 Can Plum Tomatoes, 28 oz (Drain and reserve liquid)
Tomato Juice or V8 Juice -- optional
recipeInstructions:
This is THE one you'll want to make again and again!!
Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Sauté the onions until translucent; add garlic and cook just until aromatic, stirring occasionally.
Drain and set aside. In a deep greased casserole dish, brown the ground chuck over high heat, breaking up clumps with a spatula or wooden spoon until no longer pink.
Add corn meal, milk, tomato solids, creamed corn, olives, salt to taste, and reserved tomato liquid (if using) to the meat mixture in the casserole. Stir well to combine.
Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Pour the mixture into the prepared casserole dish, smoothing the top with a spatula.
Bake for 1½ hours, or until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
In a small saucepan, make a roux by melting butter over medium-low heat. Stir in flour to form a paste; cook for about 2 minutes, then gradually whisk in reserved tomato liquid (if needed) and any added tomato juice.
Add chili powder, cayenne pepper, ground cumin, salt, and pepper to taste. Mix well.
Spoon servings of the pie into individual dishes. Top each serving with the chili sauce and garnish with paprika. Serve hot. Serves 6-8.
